metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4144
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Using super-resolution, dual-color stimulated emission depletion (STED) microscopy of neurons labelled with antibodies against CtBP1, the SV protein Syt1 and Dynamin1, the authors show that CtBP1 labelling co-localizes with Dynamin1, the classic endocytic machinery (Figure 5A). This staining is done in cells fixed 30 s after stimulation with 200 APs at 40 Hz (Figure S5). In this dataset, authors quantify the distance between CtBP1 puncta and dynamin (S5A) or Syt1 (S5B).

18/01/2021 Dnyanada: CtBP1 occurs in nuclear and cytoplasmic pools of the brain and cultured neurons and is suggested to be shuttling between the nucleus and cytoplasm (PMID:25652077). The current paper demonstrates its dynamic interaction with Dynamin1, a key player of the endocytic machinery. Based on this, the term "extrinsic component of presynaptic endocytic zone membrane" was chosen.. Experimental description: Wistar rats, (...) were used to obtain tissues and cells used in this study.
